title: Fix obj_dir reused when upgrading versions
body:
Hi there, when installing verilator on macOS via homebrew, the Verilator version output shows UNKNOWN.REV. e.g. with the latest version on homebrew:
```
Verilator 5.042 2025-11-02 rev UNKNOWN.REV
```
It seems the rev is derived from the git tag and any changes since the tag. Since the archive downloaded by the [homebrew formula](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/fdb04ba0fe695818f694a33d96572cffa187a7fd/Formula/v/verilator.rb#L4) does not include the git information, the rev comes back as unknown.

I believe this gives rise to a problem when upgrading verilator versions through homebrew where files in the obj_dir are incompatible with the new version, but verilator does not know (since UNKNOWN.REV still matches with UNKNOWN.REV?). The obj_dir must be deleted manually before running again.

Any way around this for the homebrew version to work normally without the manual deletion? Can the version check fail if the version is UNKNOWN.REV so the existing obj_dir is discarded instead of used?
